content.full_text:
State of California

Fair Political Practices Commission

P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1160 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement • • Statement of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5662          322-5660          322-5901          322-6441          322-6444


June 5, 1981

Kelly Kimball
California Coalition to
Stop the Peripheral Canal
1107 Ninth Street, Suite 1023
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Mr. Kimball:

This is to confirm our telephone conversation regarding
the obligations of the Coalition to file threshold statements
under Government Code Section 84200(b).

As I indicated to you in our telephone conversation,
there is a discrepancy between the requirements contained in
Government Code Section 84200(b) and the information contained
in the "1981 Information Manual on Campaign Disclosure Pro-
visions of the Political Reform Act." It is the Commission's
policy that in the event such a discrepancy occurs, a person
who has received a correct interpretation of the law from the
Commission's staff is required to comply with the staff's
advice, rather than with the information contained in the
manual.

Government Code Section 84200(b) states that in addition
to the semi-annual campaign statements due on July 31 and
January 31, which are required if there has been any activity
during the half-year covered, campaign statements reporting
activity during a threshold period may also be required.
Elected officers, candidates, and their controlled committees,
and committees primarily formed to support or oppose a candi-
date, are excepted from this required reporting under Section
84200(b)(4) which states that:

Notwithstanding the above, elected officers,
candidates, and their controlled committees and
committees formed primarily to support or oppose
a candidate whose activities within a threshold
period consist only of receiving contributions
and making expenditures solely in connection with
their own or their candidates campaigns are not
required to file campaign statements pursuant to
this subdivision during any six-month period (from

Kelly Kimbull                                                Page 2


                    January 1 to June 30 or from July 1 to
                    December 31) in which the candidate's
                    office is not to be voted on....

     Since the Coalition is a committee formed primarily to
support or oppose a ballot measure, it does not come under
the provisions for committees described under Section 84200
(b)(4).  Therefore, the Coalition must file campaign state-
ments for the threshold periods if it has met the single
criterion--making expenditures of $500 or more or making or
receiving contributions of $500 or more during a threshold
period.

     Please contact me if you have any additional questions.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]
                                        Jeanne Pritchard
                                        Political Reform Consultant

sections.question_synthetic: What are the threshold statement filing obligations for a ballot measure committee under Government Code Section 84200(b)?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Ballot measure committees like the Coalition must file campaign statements for threshold periods if they make expenditures of $500 or more or make/receive contributions of $500 or more during a threshold period, as they do not qualify for the exemption in Section 84200(b)(4) which applies only to candidate and elected officer committees.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION section headers; extracted question and conclusion from letter body content. Letter confirms prior telephone conversation and clarifies discrepancy between statute and information manual. Some OCR artifacts present in document text.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: What are the threshold statement filing obligations for a ballot measure committee under Government Code Section 84200(b)?

CONCLUSION: Ballot measure committees like the Coalition must file campaign statements for threshold periods if they make expenditures of $500 or more or make/receive contributions of $500 or more during a threshold period, as they do not qualify for the exemption in Section 84200(b)(4) which applies only to candidate and elected officer committees.

As I indicated to you in our telephone conversation, there is a discrepancy between the requirements contained in Government Code Section 84200(b) and the information contained in the "1981 Information Manual on Campaign Disclosure Pro- visions of the Political Reform Act." It is the Commission's policy that in the event such a discrepancy occurs, a person who has received a correct interpretation of the law from the Commission's staff is required to comply with the staff's advice, rather than with the information contained in the manual. Government Code Section 84200(b) states that in addition to the semi-annual campaign statements due on July 31 and January 31, which are required if there has been any activity during the half-year covered, campaign statements reporting activity during a threshold period may also be required. Elected officers, candidates, and their controlled committees, and committees primarily formed to support or oppose a candi- date, are excepted from this required reporting under Section 84200(b)(4) which states that: Notwithstanding the above, elected officers, candidates, and their controlled committees and committees formed primarily to support or oppose a candidate whose activities within a threshold period consist only of receiving contributions and making expenditures solely in connection with their own or their candidates campaigns are not required to file campaign statements pursuant to this subdivision during any six-month period (from Kelly Kimbull Page 2 January 1 to June 30 or from July 1 to December 31) in which the candidate's office is not to be voted on.... Since the Coalition is a committee formed primarily to support or oppose a ballot measure, it does not come under the provisions for committees described under Section 84200 (b)(4). Therefore, the Coalition must file campaign state- ments for the threshold periods if it has met the single criterion--making expenditures of $500 or more or making or receiving contributions of $500 or more during a threshold period. Please contact me if you have any additional questions. Sincerely, [signature] Jeanne Pritchard Political Reform Consultant
embedding.summary: FPPC letter to Kelly Kimball of California Coalition to Stop the Peripheral Canal clarifying threshold statement filing requirements under Government Code Section 84200(b), confirming staff telephone advice that ballot measure committees must file if they meet the $500 expenditure or contribution threshold.
